title: facility — published events
description: Every event implements `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never fire against a rolled-back write. Booking lifecycle events fan out to notifications + activity + audit; financial events (day-pass sales, pass credit consumption) additionally fan out to finance (Wave 4) for revenue attribution. BookingOverlapDetected is the CRITICAL event of the module — it means the SERIALIZABLE overlap-detection observer failed and a double-booking exists; ops must be paged immediately.

events.name: FacilityCreated
events.trigger: FacilityRepository finished a successful create. Fires exactly once per (tenant_id, branch_id, slug) tuple.

payload.name: string
payload.facility_type: string (court / field / pool / studio / room / gym_floor / equipment / lane / general)

events.name: FacilityUpdated
events.trigger: Any Facility scalar field mutated except status (FacilityStatusChanged), pricing_json (FacilityPricingUpdated), availability_json (FacilityAvailabilityChanged), and blackout_dates (FacilityBlackoutAdded / FacilityBlackoutRemoved). Umbrella event for renames, capacity changes, sport_category changes, min/max duration tweaks, advance_booking_window_days edits.

events.name: FacilityStatusChanged
events.trigger: Facility.status transitioned. Values: active / maintenance / retired. `active → maintenance` is temporary; `active → retired` is terminal (fires FacilityRetired in parallel).
consumers: facility::CancelFutureBookingsOnMaintenance (Wave 2b — dispatched only on maintenance transition; retired path is a separate flow)

events.name: FacilityPricingUpdated
events.trigger: Facility.pricing_json JSONB blob mutated. Any sub-field change (model, currency, tiers[], peak_hours) fires this event. Financial-record retention 7 years applies to the resulting audit row.

events.name: FacilityBlackoutAdded
events.trigger: A blackout window was appended to Facility.blackout_dates. Fires per blackout added — a multi-blackout admin update emits N events.
consumers: facility::CancelBookingsInBlackoutWindow (cascades: finds bookings inside the window + fires BookingCancelled on each)

payload.blackout_id: string (opaque handle for the JSON array element)
payload.affected_booking_count: integer (bookings inside the window at the time of add; cascaded to BookingCancelled)

events.name: FacilityBlackoutRemoved
events.trigger: A blackout window was removed from Facility.blackout_dates. Does NOT restore cancelled bookings — those are gone.

events.name: FacilityAvailabilityChanged
events.trigger: Facility.availability_json mutated. Distinct from FacilityBlackout* (recurring weekly hours vs one-off windows). Observer refuses saves whose new availability window is broader than the parent Branch.opening_hours.

events.name: FacilityRetired
events.trigger: Facility.status transitioned to `retired` OR facility soft-delete completed with the `--cascade-bookings` flag. Terminal. Fires alongside FacilityStatusChanged.
consumers: facility::CancelFutureBookingsOnFacilityRetired (cascades: cancels every pending/confirmed booking with starts_at > now; fires BookingCancelled per booking)

events.name: BookingCreated
events.trigger: ResourceBookingObserver committed a new resource_bookings row. Fires ONCE per booking. Precedes BookingConfirmed for pending-status bookings; for auto-confirmed bookings the two events fire in the same transaction.

events.name: BookingConfirmed
events.trigger: ResourceBooking.status transitioned pending → confirmed. Fires when the payment/gate approval flow (Wave 4 Finance approval, or immediate on free bookings) resolves. Also fires directly at BookingCreated time for auto-confirmed bookings — the two events are dispatched in the same afterCommit hook, in that order.

events.name: BookingCancelled
events.trigger: ResourceBooking.status transitioned to `cancelled` — either voluntarily by the booker/admin (via the cancel action) OR cascaded by FacilityRetired / FacilityBlackoutAdded. The `cancellation_reason` payload field distinguishes the two.
consumers: finance::MaybeRefund (Wave 4 — refund logic evaluates cancellation_notice_hours + payment_status + admin override)

events.name: BookingCompleted
events.trigger: ResourceBooking.status transitioned confirmed → completed. Fired ONLY by the automated `MarkBookingCompletedJob` when ends_at + 15 min has passed AND the booking wasn't cancelled or no-show. Never fires from a manual admin action (observer refuses manual transitions to completed).
consumers: finance::RecognizeBookingRevenue (Wave 4 — moves pending revenue to recognised)

events.name: BookingMarkedNoShow
events.trigger: ResourceBooking.status transitioned to `no_show`. Fired by the automated `MarkNoShowJob` when starts_at + 15 min has passed with no check-in AND the booking is still confirmed. Also fired by manual admin action.
consumers: finance::MaybeRetainPayment (Wave 4 — most tenants keep the full charge on no-show)

events.name: BookingRescheduled
events.trigger: ResourceBooking.starts_at / ends_at mutated on an in-flight booking. Rescheduling into a conflicting slot is refused by the observer (overlap detection runs on reschedule too). Rescheduling triggers a fresh overlap check + capacity check + availability check.

events.name: BookingOverlapDetected
events.trigger: CRITICAL — the SERIALIZABLE overlap-detection observer detected a double-booking after the fact. Fires ONLY as a paranoia safety net (e.g. a raw SQL write bypassed the observer, or a race in a non-SERIALIZABLE transaction slipped through). Every fire indicates ops attention is required. This event exists so alerting has a first-class signal to hook — it should NEVER fire in production.
consumers: observability::MetricsCollector (increments stackra.facility.overlaps.detected — this counter should stay at 0)

events.name: BookingCheckedIn
events.trigger: Booker or receptionist called the check-in action. Sets checked_in_at on the booking. Distinct from BookingCompleted (check-in is at start-time; completion is at end-time + 15 min).

events.name: DayPassIssued
events.trigger: DayPassRepository committed a new day_passes row from the POS surface. Fires exactly once per row.

events.name: DayPassConsumed
events.trigger: DayPass.consumed_at set from null → now(). Fires on turnstile scan or receptionist manual confirmation.

events.name: DayPassExpired
events.trigger: ExpireDayPassesJob transitioned a DayPass past valid_on_date without consumption. No refund flow triggered — day-pass revenue was already recognised on purchase.

events.name: DayPassRefunded
events.trigger: Manual refund action from staff (Wave 4 refund flow reversal). Requires DayPass.consumed_at IS NULL AND staff has facility.day_passes.refund permission.

payload.refund_amount_cents: integer (may differ from price_amount_cents on partial refunds)

events.name: PassIssued
events.trigger: PassProvisioner committed a new passes row. Fires either from a Wave 4 Membership provisioning listener OR from manual admin action.

payload.facility_id: ULID | null (null = valid at any facility in the branch)
payload.membership_id: ULID | null (Wave 4 — null in Wave 2 placeholder rows)

events.name: PassCreditConsumed
events.trigger: Pass.used_credits incremented by 1 during BookingCreated (when the booking booked_for_user has an active pass covering this facility). The observer atomically increments in the same transaction as the booking-create — a failure to consume aborts the booking.

payload.consumed_credits: integer (always 1 in Wave 2 — bulk consumption reserved for future)
payload.consuming_booking_id: ULID | null (null = day-pass consumption if we later route DayPass through Pass)

events.name: PassExhausted
events.trigger: Pass.used_credits reached Pass.total_credits. Fires from PassObserver.updated when the increment crosses the threshold. NOT fired on subsequent increments (there won't be any — the observer refuses over-consumption).

events.name: PassExpired
events.trigger: ExpirePassesJob transitioned a pass past valid_until (with unused credits — expired-and-unused). Refunds handled by Finance module case-by-case, not automatic.
consumers: notifications::DispatchPassExpiringNotification (dispatched 7 days BEFORE expiry — this event fires ON expiry)

events.name: PassTransferred
events.trigger: Pass.assignable_user_ids mutated OR the primary owner-of-record on the pass changed. Refused when pass.is_transferable=false.

events.name: PassRevoked
events.trigger: Admin invoked the revoke action. Sets deleted_at + revocation_reason. Distinct from PassExhausted (used-up) and PassExpired (time-up). Refund handling is Finance module's job.
consumers: notifications::DispatchPassRevokedNotification (cannot-opt-out — the pass owner needs to know)
